w:r.w:t: Smart College Event Fund Collection System
Ansari Abdul Raheman Gufran Ahmed (Roll No: 2501)
form part of any other course undergone by the candidate.
I am pleased to present the report for the
Smart College Event Fund Collection System
which I have undertaken as part of my academic curriculum.
I would like to express my sincere gratitude to [
Name of the Mentor, Guide, and Director
] for her/his invaluable guidance and unwavering support throughout this endeavor.
I am also grateful to all those who have contributed to the success of this project in accordance with the guidelines set forth by Mumbai University.
Ansari Abdul Raheman Gufran Ahmed (Roll No: 2501)

is a web application built on the MERN (MongoDB, Express.js, React.js, Node.js) stack that revolutionizes the way college events and activities are funded. The traditional method of collecting funds for college events—charity drives, annual functions, cultural events, and other activities—has always been manual and inefficient.
Currently, most colleges use WhatsApp QR codes to collect payments from students for various events. This approach presents numerous challenges:
: Event organizers cannot easily track who has paid and who hasn't
: Payments need to be verified manually by checking bank transactions or UPI apps
: There is no single source of truth for payment information
: Students may accidentally submit duplicate payment details, causing confusion
: Computing class-wise and event-wise totals requires manual counting
solves these problems by providing a comprehensive digital platform where:
Students can easily submit payment details through an intuitive interface
Event organizers can automatically receive email notifications for each payment
The system provides real-time dashboard analytics showing verified and pending payments
Fund verification is simplified by matching UPI sender names and transaction IDs
Class-wise and event-wise collection totals are generated automatically

: Fundraising for events in colleges is predominantly done through manual processes. Students transfer money to organizers via UPI using WhatsApp-shared QR codes or by direct bank transfers without proper documentation.

: Without an adequate tracking system, it is challenging to determine which student or class has made a payment. Organizers maintain scattered records across multiple platforms (WhatsApp groups, personal notes, spreadsheets).
: Organizers struggle to verify each payment as they must manually cross-check every transaction with their UPI transaction history or bank statements.

: The absence of a centralized platform creates transparency concerns. Students do not have confirmation that their payment was recorded, and organizers cannot provide class-wise or event-wise collection summaries easily.
: Manual record-keeping leads to confusion, data loss, duplicate entries, and poor fund management. Important payment information can be missed or incorrectly recorded.
: As the number of events and students increases, manual management becomes increasingly difficult and time-consuming.

: There is an urgent need for an automated, transparent, and efficient digital platform that streamlines college fund collection, automates payment verification, provides real-time analytics, and maintains complete transparency between students and event organizers.
Create a Digital Platform for Transparent Fund Management
Establish a centralized web-based platform accessible to all college members
Ensure all transactions are recorded in a unified database
Provide complete transparency in the fund collection process
Allow students to easily select events and submit payment details
Support multiple payment methods with emphasis on UPI (Unified Payments Interface)
Generate automatic UPI payment links for quick transactions
Provide confirmation messages to students after successful submission
Send real-time email notifications to event administrators upon each payment submission
Include comprehensive payment details in notification emails
Provide one-click verification links in email notifications for seamless verification
Display all verified and pending payments in a user-friendly dashboard
Show real-time payment status with clear visual indicators
Allow filtering of payments by event, class, and status
Match student-submitted UPI sender names with transaction records
Verify UPI transaction IDs with admin UPI app records
Reduce manual verification effort through smart matching algorithms
Provide visual reports and analytics for better decision-making
Track collection progress against target amounts
Implement secure authentication for all users
operates on a three-tier architecture comprising:
: React.js-based user interface for students and administrators
: Node.js + Express.js API server for business logic
: MongoDB for secure and scalable data storage
Login to the admin dashboard with credentials
Create a new fundraising event with event details and UPI ID
Set target collection amount and event deadline
Monitor payments in real-time through the dashboard
Receive email notifications for each payment submission
Verify payments by checking UPI transaction details and approving through email or dashboard
View class-wise and event-wise collection summaries
Select an event to contribute to
Enter personal details (name, class, roll number, email)
Specify payment amount and UPI sender name
Click "Pay Now" to generate a UPI payment link
Complete payment through their UPI app (
Submit the form to confirm payment
Receive confirmation email with payment details
Event creation and management by admins
Student payment submission with UPI integration
One-click payment verification through email links
Secure user authentication using JWT tokens
Input validation on frontend and backend
Secure email verification links with expiration
Responsive design for desktop and mobile devices
Real-time form validation with error messages
Student → Payment Portal → Select Event → Enter Details → UPI Payment
Payment Confirmation → Form Submission → Database Storage
Email Notification → Admin Dashboard → Verify Payment → Update Status
Analytics & Reports → Class-wise Summary → Event-wise Summary
: Intel Core i3 or higher / AMD Ryzen 3 or equivalent
: 512 GB SSD minimum for faster development and compilation
: 15" or larger screen for comfortable development
: Minimum 50 GB for initial data storage
: Broadband connection with minimum 5 Mbps upload speed
: React.js (v18.x or latest) – For building interactive user interfaces
: Tailwind CSS – For responsive and modern UI design
: Vite or Create React App – For development and production build
: React Context API – For managing application state
: React Hook Form – For efficient form handling
: React Router – For navigation between pages
: Google Chrome, Firefox, Safari, or Edge
: Node.js (v16.x or higher) – JavaScript runtime for server-side code
: Express.js – Lightweight web framework for REST API development
: Joi or express-validator – For input validation
: Mongoose for schema definition and database operations
: Git & GitHub for source code management
: Heroku, Render, Railway, or AWS EC2
: GitHub for code repository and team collaboration
: Notion or Confluence for project documentation
: Slack or Discord for team communication
Component-based architecture for reusable UI elements
Unidirectional data flow with props and state
Tailwind CSS for responsive design and styling
: One-to-many (One admin can create multiple events)
: One-to-many (One event can have multiple payments)
: Many-to-one (Multiple admins can verify multiple payments)
: One-to-many (One payment can generate multiple email logs)
is built on proven, industry-standard technologies:
React.js is widely adopted for building interactive web applications
Node.js + Express.js is the industry standard for REST API development
MongoDB provides flexible, scalable data storage
All technologies have extensive documentation and active communities
is a reliable solution for sending transactional emails
Free SMTP services available (Gmail SMTP, SendGrid free tier)
Email templates can be easily customized using HTML and CSS
UPI payment links can be generated without payment gateway integration
Students use their existing UPI apps (
No PCI compliance required as no card data is handled
Cloud deployment options (Heroku, Render, AWS) support auto-scaling
Caching strategies can be implemented for frequently accessed data
React provides fast client-side rendering with virtual DOM
Database queries can be optimized with proper indexing
CDN can be used for static asset delivery
No experimental or beta technologies are used
Development team has experience with MERN stack
Clear development roadmap and achievable milestones
Simple 3-step process: Select Event → Enter Details → Pay
Familiar UPI interface requiring no new learning
Intuitive dashboard with clear visual indicators
Automated email alerts reduce manual monitoring
Cloud-based hosting eliminates server maintenance overhead
Automated database backups through MongoDB Atlas
Easy deployment and updates through CI/CD pipelines
Monitoring tools available for system health checks
Minimal training needed for students (intuitive interface)
Admin training: 2-3 hours for dashboard and verification process
Video tutorials can be created for self-learning
No integration with existing college systems required
Standalone application, can be introduced independently
Future integration with college ERP is possible
No payment gateway fees (free UPI links)
Reduced staff time for fund management
Can be monetized through premium features for other colleges
White-label version for NGOs and community organizations
Sponsorship opportunities from payment service providers
Scalable to multiple colleges with minimal additional cost
One-time development investment yields long-term benefits
All technologies are open-source and free
Cloud services offer free tiers sufficient for college-scale operations
No vendor lock-in; can migrate to other platforms if needed
Complete digital record of all transactions
Easy audit trail for financial compliance
Enhanced trust between students and organizers
Confirmation of payment receipt (reduced anxiety about missing funds)
Fair and transparent distribution of collected funds
Significant reduction in manual work and paperwork
Automatic calculation of class-wise and event-wise totals
Quick identification of non-payers for follow-up
Improved event planning with accurate funding information
Promotes digital literacy among college members
Demonstrates real-world application of web technologies
Encourages students to adopt digital solutions for other challenges
Supports India's digital revolution and cashless economy vision
Eliminates printed forms, receipts, and documents
Reduces carbon footprint through paperless operations
Supports sustainability goals of educational institutions
Can be extended to other colleges with minimal customization
Applicable to NGOs, community organizations, and donation drives
Model can be adapted for school fee collection, hostel fees, etc.
Demonstrates technology's role in social good
Works on any smartphone with internet connectivity
No special hardware or software required
Supports India's vast UPI user base
Free to use for students and basic admin functions
Simple interface suitable for all tech-savviness levels
Multilingual support can be added for diverse user base
Secure storage of student payment information
Limited data sharing with proper consent
Introduces college members to digital payment systems
Prepares students for digital financial literacy
Automated verification eliminates manual handling of funds
Creates a replicable model for schools, universities, and organizations
Contributes to digital transformation in education sector
Inspires other IT projects addressing similar challenges
Select event and enter payment information
Create new fundraising event with UPI ID
Check UPI transaction and approve payment
Monitor all payments for their events
Automatically send payment alerts to admin
is divided into five primary modules, each handling specific functionalities:
: Manage user login, registration, and access control
: Handle creation, management, and retrieval of fundraising events
Update event status and collected amount
: Handle student payment submissions and admin verification
Query payments with filters (status, date range, class, event)
: Send automated email notifications to students and admins
: Generate reports and analytics for administrators
email: { type: String, unique: true, required: true },
Admin receives email with verify link
Collected amount updated, pending list refreshed
Class-wise total updated, dashboard reflects change
1. Browse events 2. Select event 3. Fill form 4. Pay
Event created and visible in list
Allow admins to add multiple UPI IDs for payment collection
Support for google pay business accounts
QR code generation for easy sharing
Direct payment processing without manual UPI
Export reports in multiple formats (PDF, Excel, CSV)
Regional language support for wider adoption
Native Android app using React Native
Implement Redis caching for frequently accessed data
Database query optimization with better indexing
Regular security audits and penetration testing
Database sharding for large data sets
Link with hostel fee and tuition fee systems
Automatic notifications to parents for payments
Integration with Google Workspace for calendar sync
Compliance with university fund management guidelines
Support for community festivals and events

represents a comprehensive solution to the long-standing problem of manual college fund management. This project successfully demonstrates the application of modern web technologies (MERN stack) to solve real-world administrative challenges in educational institutions.
: Transformed manual, error-prone fund collection into an automated, transparent, and efficient digital process.
: Created intuitive interfaces for both students and administrators, making the system accessible to users with varying technical expertise.
: Built on a scalable architecture capable of handling hundreds of concurrent users and thousands of transactions.
Reduced manual workload for event organizers
Through this project, the developer has gained expertise in:
Security best practices in web applications
Deployment and hosting on cloud platforms
The project has been validated as:
: All technologies are production-ready and well-documented
: Easy to deploy and maintain with minimal technical intervention
: Low development and operational costs with potential for monetization
: Promotes transparency, reduces corruption, and encourages digitalization
While the current implementation provides a solid foundation, the system is designed for easy extension with:

successfully addresses the identified problem statement and delivers a production-ready solution that can be immediately deployed in educational institutions. The project demonstrates the value of thoughtful software design, user-centered development, and modern technology implementation in solving institutional challenges. With its clean architecture, comprehensive documentation, and built-in extensibility,
serves as both a practical tool and a template for similar applications in other sectors.
MERN Stack Tutorial: Build Full-Stack JavaScript Applications
Architectural Styles and the Design of Network-based Software Architectures

This detailed project report has been prepared in accordance with the University of Mumbai's MCA project guidelines and follows the prescribed format structure.
